choice: Implement the real SSA PIA engine (AIME from top-35 wage-indexed earnings → bend-point PIA → claiming-age adjustment to 70 → CPI-W COLA), but let earnings be supplied cheaply: project from current salary by default, with optional full earnings history for accuracy. Allow the user to override the whole calc with their SSA-statement FRA estimate, which short-circuits the PIA step and applies only the claiming-age adjustment + COLA. Generic streams keep the `{inflation | as-is | fixed-yoy}` COLA enum and a tax/deferral rule; SS forces CPI-W COLA. Separately, confirm accumulation-phase modeling (income → deferral + tax → post-tax expense offset → withdrawal of the shortfall) is in scope for this engine.
rationale: The user explicitly asked for 'the model the SSA uses,' which is the PIA/bend-point/claiming-adjustment chain — a flat dollar amount would misstate claiming-age and COLA dynamics and break survivor-benefit handling. But requiring a full 35-year earnings history is a heavy input burden, so salary-projection and a statement-estimate override keep it usable without abandoning the SSA structure. The 401(k)/post-tax coupling the user introduced turns this from a pure offset into a lifecycle model, so its scope must be pinned before the stream schema is finalized.
